sections.title: Overview
sections.content:
The Okta connector integrates with the **Okta REST API v1** to sync identity and access management data into the Shielva knowledge base. It is designed for IT, security, and platform teams that manage user lifecycle, group membership, application SSO, and audit trails in Okta.

**What it syncs:**
- **Users** — all Okta user profiles, including status, department, title, login, email, phone, and activation/last-login timestamps
- **Groups** — all Okta groups with names, descriptions, types, and membership timestamps
- **Applications** — all Okta application integrations (SSO apps), including sign-on mode, status, features, and self-service enrollment settings
- **System logs** — Okta system log events including authentication events, admin actions, policy changes, and MFA events

**Who uses it:** IT admins and security teams that manage identity in Okta and need user/group/app records and audit log events surfaced in Shielva for compliance, access review, and security analytics.

**Key capabilities:**
- SSWS token authentication — `Authorization: SSWS {api_token}` header on every request
- Cursor-based pagination via HTTP `Link: rel=next` header for all resource types
- `since` parameter support on log fetches for incremental log ingestion
- Separate page-size caps per resource: 200 users/groups/apps, 100 log events per page
- `GET /api/v1/users/me` health check to verify token validity and identify the authenticated user
- SHA-256 stable document IDs keyed on entity type and Okta ID
- Automatic retry with exponential back-off (max 3 attempts) with 429 rate-limit support
- Multi-tenant: every document stamped with `connector_id` and `tenant_id`

sections.title: Quick Start
sections.content:
### Prerequisites
- An Okta organization (any tier)
- **Read-Only Administrator** role (minimum) or custom role with `okta.users.read`, `okta.groups.read`, `okta.apps.read`, and `okta.logs.read` permissions

### Step 1 — Create an Okta API Token

1. Log in to your Okta Admin Console at `https://{your-domain}/admin`
2. Go to **Security** → **API** → **Tokens**
3. Click **Create Token**
4. Enter a name (e.g. `Shielva Connector`)
5. Click **Create Token** and copy it — the token is shown only once

### Step 2 — Find your Okta domain

Your Okta domain appears in the Admin Console URL bar: `https://{domain}/admin/`. Examples:
- `dev-123456.okta.com` (developer org)
- `company.okta.com` (production org)
- `company.oktapreview.com` (preview/sandbox org)

### Step 3 — Install the connector

```python
import asyncio
from connector import OktaConnector

connector = OktaConnector(
    tenant_id="tenant-abc",
    connector_id="okta-01",
    config={
        "api_token": "00abc_DEF-...",
        "domain": "dev-123456.okta.com",
    },
)
result = asyncio.run(connector.install())
print(result.health, result.message)
# ConnectorHealth.HEALTHY  Connected to Okta
```

### Step 4 — Verify health

```python
health = asyncio.run(connector.health_check())
print(health.health, health.message)
# ConnectorHealth.HEALTHY  Okta API is reachable. Authenticated as admin@company.com
```

### Step 5 — Run your first sync

```python
result = asyncio.run(connector.sync(kb_id="my-knowledge-base"))
print(f"{result.status}: {result.documents_synced}/{result.documents_found} records synced")
# SyncStatus.COMPLETED: 2847/2847 records synced
```

sections.title: Authentication
sections.content:
The Okta connector uses an **SSWS API Token** — no OAuth flow. The token is sent on every request as a custom `Authorization` header.

### Auth header format

```
Authorization: SSWS 00abc_DEF-xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx
```

### API base URL

```
https://{domain}/api/v1/
```

The `domain` field in config is used verbatim to construct the base URL. Do not include `https://` or trailing slashes in the domain value.

### Validation endpoint

The connector validates credentials by calling `GET /api/v1/users/me`. A successful response confirms the token is valid and returns the profile of the token's owner. A `401` raises `OktaAuthError` with `INVALID_CREDENTIALS`.

### Token revocation

Okta API tokens do not expire automatically, but they are revoked if:
- The token is manually revoked in Security → API → Tokens
- The token's owning user is deactivated or deleted
- The token's owning user's role is downgraded below the required permissions

### Required permissions

The token's owning user must have one of:
- **Read-Only Administrator** role (minimum, grants all read access)
- OR a custom role with: `okta.users.read`, `okta.groups.read`, `okta.apps.read`, and `okta.logs.read`

**Note:** System log access (`/api/v1/logs`) requires at least Read-Only Administrator. Custom roles may not include log access depending on your Okta edition.

sections.title: Configuration
sections.content:
Pass all fields in the `config` dict when constructing `OktaConnector`.

| Field | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| `api_token` | password | Yes | Okta SSWS API token. Created in Okta Admin Console: Security → API → Tokens → Create Token. Format: `00abc_DEF-...`. Stored encrypted by Shielva. |
| `domain` | string | Yes | Your Okta organization domain without `https://` or trailing slash. Examples: `dev-123456.okta.com`, `company.okta.com`, `company.oktapreview.com`. |

sections.title: API Methods
sections.content: All methods are `async` and must be awaited. `OktaConnector` supports the async context manager protocol (`async with OktaConnector(...) as c:`). The HTTP client is lazily initialized via `_ensure_client()` on first use.

children.title: install()
children.content:
Validates that `api_token` and `domain` are both present, then calls `GET /api/v1/users/me` to verify the token is valid. On success, reinitializes the instance client.

**Returns:** `InstallResult`
- `health`: `ConnectorHealth.HEALTHY` or `OFFLINE`
- `auth_status`: `AuthStatus.CONNECTED`, `MISSING_CREDENTIALS`, `INVALID_CREDENTIALS`, or `FAILED`
- `connector_id`: echoed from constructor
- `message`: `'Connected to Okta'` on success

```python
connector = OktaConnector(
    tenant_id="tenant-abc",
    connector_id="okta-01",
    config={"api_token": "00abc_DEF-...", "domain": "dev-123456.okta.com"},
)
result = await connector.install()
# InstallResult(health=HEALTHY, auth_status=CONNECTED, message='Connected to Okta')
```

children.title: health_check()
children.content:
Pings `GET /api/v1/users/me` and returns the login of the authenticated user in the message. Distinguishes between auth failures (`OktaAuthError`) and network failures (`OktaNetworkError`).

**Returns:** `HealthCheckResult`
- `health`: `HEALTHY`, `DEGRADED`, or `OFFLINE`
- `auth_status`: `CONNECTED`, `MISSING_CREDENTIALS`, `INVALID_CREDENTIALS`, or `FAILED`
- `message`: includes authenticated login on success, e.g. `'Okta API is reachable. Authenticated as admin@company.com'`
- `username`: the authenticated user's Okta login

```python
health = await connector.health_check()
if health.health.value == "healthy":
    print(f"Connected as: {health.username}")
```

children.title: sync()
children.content:
Performs a full sync of Okta users, groups, applications, and system logs in sequence. A fatal failure on the users fetch returns `SyncStatus.FAILED` immediately (users are the primary resource). Group, app, and log fetch failures increment `documents_failed` by 1 and continue the sync.

**Parameters:**
- `kb_id` (str, optional) — knowledge base ID to ingest documents into
- `**kwargs` — ignored (forward compatibility)

**Returns:** `SyncResult`
- `status`: `SyncStatus.COMPLETED` (zero failures) or `PARTIAL` or `FAILED`
- `documents_found`: total records across all resource types
- `documents_synced`: successfully normalized and ingested
- `documents_failed`: normalization failures or fetch failures (groups/apps/logs)

```python
result = await connector.sync(kb_id="identity-kb")
print(result.status, result.documents_synced, result.documents_found)
# SyncStatus.COMPLETED 2847 2847
```

children.title: list_users()
children.content:
Fetches **all** Okta users by following the cursor extracted from the `Link: rel=next` response header until exhausted. Page size is `SYNC_USER_LIMIT` (200 per page).

**Parameters:**
- `**kwargs` — extra filter params forwarded to `GET /api/v1/users` (e.g. `filter`, `search`, `q`)

**Returns:** `List[Dict]` — flat list of all user dicts

Key user fields: `id`, `status`, `created`, `activated`, `lastLogin`, `lastUpdated`, and a `profile` sub-dict with `login`, `email`, `firstName`, `lastName`, `department`, `title`, `mobilePhone`, `organization`.

```python
users = await connector.list_users()
print(f"Found {len(users)} users")
for u in users[:5]:
    print(u["profile"]["login"], u["status"])

# Filter to ACTIVE users only:
active = await connector.list_users(filter='status eq "ACTIVE"')
```

children.title: get_user()
children.content:
Fetches a single Okta user by their Okta user ID or login.

**Parameters:**
- `user_id` (str, required) — Okta user ID (e.g. `00u1a2b3c4d5e6f7g`) or login (e.g. `user@company.com`)

**Returns:** `Dict` — full user resource

```python
user = await connector.get_user(user_id="00u1a2b3c4d5e6f7g")
print(user["profile"]["firstName"], user["status"])

# Also works with login:
user = await connector.get_user(user_id="admin@company.com")
```

children.title: list_groups()
children.content:
Fetches **all** Okta groups by following the `Link: rel=next` cursor. Page size is `SYNC_GROUP_LIMIT` (200 per page).

**Parameters:** none

**Returns:** `List[Dict]`

Key group fields: `id`, `type` (`OKTA_GROUP`, `APP_GROUP`, `BUILT_IN`), `created`, `lastUpdated`, `lastMembershipUpdated`, and a `profile` sub-dict with `name` and `description`.

```python
groups = await connector.list_groups()
for g in groups:
    print(g["profile"]["name"], g["type"])
```

children.title: list_apps()
children.content:
Fetches **all** Okta application integrations by following the `Link: rel=next` cursor. Page size is `SYNC_APP_LIMIT` (200 per page).

**Parameters:** none

**Returns:** `List[Dict]`

Key app fields: `id`, `name` (internal name), `label` (display name), `status`, `signOnMode` (SAML_2_0, OIDC_CLIENT, WS_FEDERATION, etc.), `created`, `lastUpdated`, `features`, and `accessibility.selfService`.

```python
apps = await connector.list_apps()
for app in apps:
    print(app["label"], app["signOnMode"], app["status"])
```

children.title: list_logs()
children.content:
Fetches Okta system log events by following the `Link: rel=next` cursor. Page size is `SYNC_LOG_LIMIT` (100 per page). Supports `since` for incremental fetches.

**Parameters:**
- `since` (str, optional) — ISO 8601 datetime string; only events after this time are returned (e.g. `"2024-06-01T00:00:00Z"`)
- `**kwargs` — extra filter params (e.g. `until`, `filter`, `q`)

**Returns:** `List[Dict]`

Key log fields: `uuid`, `published`, `eventType`, `displayMessage`, `severity`, `outcome` (result + reason), `actor` (displayName, type), `client` (ipAddress, userAgent), `target` (list of affected resources).

```python
# All logs:
logs = await connector.list_logs()

# Incremental since last run:
recent_logs = await connector.list_logs(since="2024-06-19T00:00:00Z")
for log in recent_logs:
    print(log["eventType"], log["outcome"]["result"], log["actor"]["displayName"])
```

sections.title: Error Handling
sections.content:
Typed exceptions are defined in `exceptions.py`. All HTTP calls are wrapped with `with_retry()` from `helpers.py`.

### Exception classes

| Exception | HTTP status | Meaning | Connector behaviour |
|-----------|-------------|---------|---------------------|
| `OktaAuthError` | 401, 403 | Invalid token or insufficient permissions | `install()` returns `OFFLINE / INVALID_CREDENTIALS`; `health_check()` returns `OFFLINE / INVALID_CREDENTIALS`; not retried |
| `OktaNetworkError` | Network-level | Connection timeout, DNS failure | `health_check()` returns `DEGRADED / FAILED`; retried up to 3 times |
| `OktaError` | 4xx / 5xx | General API error (rate limit, not found, server error) | Users: returns `FAILED` immediately; groups/apps/logs: increments `documents_failed` and continues |

### Retry behaviour
- `with_retry()` retries on `OktaNetworkError` and 5xx responses
- 429 rate-limit responses are retried (Okta sends `Retry-After` header, respected by back-off logic)
- `OktaAuthError` is never retried
- Base delay: 1 second, back-off factor: 2x, max delay: 30 seconds, max 3 attempts

### Okta rate limits
Okta enforces per-org per-endpoint rate limits. Developer orgs: ~600 requests/minute for most endpoints. Production orgs: higher, but varies by tier and endpoint. The connector's retry logic handles 429s automatically.

### sync() failure modes

| Resource | Failure handling |
|----------|------------------|
| Users (fetch fails) | Entire `sync()` returns `SyncStatus.FAILED` with error message |
| Groups (fetch fails) | `documents_failed += 1`; sync continues with apps and logs |
| Apps (fetch fails) | `documents_failed += 1`; sync continues with logs |
| Logs (fetch fails) | `documents_failed += 1`; sync completes |
| Individual record normalization | `documents_failed += 1`; next record continues |

sections.title: Troubleshooting
sections.content:
### `install()` returns `MISSING_CREDENTIALS` with `'api_token is required'`
**Cause:** The `api_token` field is empty or not provided.
**Fix:** Create an API token at Okta Admin Console → Security → API → Tokens → Create Token.

### `install()` returns `MISSING_CREDENTIALS` with `'domain is required'`
**Cause:** The `domain` field is empty.
**Fix:** Provide your Okta domain without `https://` (e.g. `dev-123456.okta.com`).

### `install()` returns `INVALID_CREDENTIALS` (`OktaAuthError`)
**Cause:** The API token is invalid (wrong value, revoked, or token owner deactivated).
**Fix:** Generate a new token at Security → API → Tokens. Ensure the token owner has at least the Read-Only Administrator role.

### `health_check()` returns `OFFLINE / INVALID_CREDENTIALS`
**Cause:** The token has been revoked or the owning user's permissions have been downgraded.
**Fix:** Check Security → API → Tokens for the token status. Generate a new token if needed.

### `health_check()` returns `DEGRADED` (`OktaNetworkError`)
**Cause:** Your Okta org is unreachable (incorrect domain, network issue, or Okta outage).
**Fix:** Verify the `domain` value. Check https://status.okta.com.

### `sync()` returns `FAILED` — `'Failed to fetch users: ...'`
**Cause:** The users API call failed (likely auth or rate limit).
**Fix:** Inspect the error message. Verify the token has `okta.users.read` permission (Read-Only Admin or higher).

### System log sync returns 0 records
**Cause:** Log access requires at least Read-Only Administrator role. Custom roles may not include log access.
**Fix:** Ensure the token owner has the Read-Only Administrator role, not just a custom role. Confirm log access at Security → API → Tokens → (token owner's role).

### `list_users()` with `filter` parameter fails
**Cause:** Okta filter expressions use a specific syntax. Invalid filter syntax returns a 400.
**Fix:** Refer to the [Okta List Users filter documentation](https://developer.okta.com/docs/reference/api/users/#list-users-with-a-filter). Example valid filter: `filter='status eq "ACTIVE"'`.

### Token owner deactivated causes 401 mid-sync
**Cause:** If the API token owner's Okta account is deactivated during a sync, subsequent requests return 401.
**Fix:** Create a dedicated service account user with Read-Only Admin role to own the API token, to avoid this during offboarding.
